preamble: The voiced postalveolar affricate [dʒ] shifts to the voiced alveolar affricate [dz], representing a change in place of articulation from postalveolar to alveolar. This process is attested in historical sound change and synchronic phonological variation across Slavic and other language families.

examples.note: Historical shift from postalveolar to alveolar affrication in Proto-Slavic evolution, particularly affecting borrowed or innovated sounds.
Phonetic realization of [dʒ] as [dz] in loanwords from English or other languages, reflecting native phonological patterns.
Dialectal or rapid speech realization where postalveolar affricates are retracted or alveolarized.
